category_title: Students with mild intellectual disabilities exhibiting academic engagement, off-task, or disruptive problem behaviors
scenario_title: Disruptive problem behaviors during general instruction
minimal_context: The student is expected to listen to the teacher and answer questions during a general classroom lesson while seated near peers.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: We are going to go over the lesson now. Please sit at your desk, listen carefully, and be ready to answer questions.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Looks outside through the window and starts playing with a pencil instead of listening.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please pay attention to the lesson.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Turns around to the peer sitting at the desk behind them) Look at what I can do with my pencil.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Stop talking to your friends. You need to sit quietly and listen to me right now.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Stands up without permission, walks over to the friend's desk, and grabs their eraser while talking aloud) I am taking this!

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: We are going to go over the lesson now. Please sit at your desk, listen carefully, and be ready to answer questions.

STUDENT: (Looks outside through the window and starts playing with a pencil instead of listening.)

TUTOR: Please pay attention to the lesson.

STUDENT: (Turns around to the peer sitting at the desk behind them) Look at what I can do with my pencil.

TUTOR: Stop talking to your friends. You need to sit quietly and listen to me right now.

STUDENT: (Stands up without permission, walks over to the friend's desk, and grabs their eraser while talking aloud) I am taking this!

task.content: Listening to teacher or peer, asking questions, answering questions

context.social_setting: General classroom environment with peers/friends nearby

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: Placement in the classroom (not sitting in the front desks)
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Talking to friends irrelevant to the lesson, talking aloud, disturbing friends, hitting friends, taking her/his friends’ possessions without permission, walking in the class, standing up without permission, or acting against the teachers’ instructions

behavior_patterns.condition: When teachers manifest disapproval of students' behaviors or provide low rates of approval
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Behaviors that interrupt the lesson, disturbing or distracting the teacher or other students
